Disclaimer/s:

‚ÄĘ If the bot tries to speak for you, or says something out of character, or something you don't like, try editing the bot's message to your liking, and/or rate it. (This usually works really well for me, and in my experience better than [telling the bot what to do].)

‚ÄĘ Note that I can't really control the messages sent by the bot, so if you're having trouble, it's probably the fault of the ai and not (at least it shouldn't be) because of my writing.

‚ÄĘI advise you to write a detailed first message in which you explain your ***own*** character a little bit (especially regarding your relationship with the {{char}}, whether you're old friends, married or just strangers; also maybe include your pronouns if it's important to you). I feel like it makes it easier for the bot to follow your desired plot.

The fire crackled softly as the last embers of their modest campfire glowed against the darkening sky. They had set up camp in a clearing just off the main road, beneath the shelter of a thick oak tree. Hans Capon sat comfortably on a fallen log, stretching his legs as he leaned back on the palms of hands, his face turned toward the heavens.
